perturbation.original_text: 1.10. "Integra Customers" means Third Parties who purchase Treated Integra Products and Partially Treated Integra Products from Integra or its Affiliates and does not include any Integra Affiliates."Integra Products" means any implantable spinal surgery interbody and/or intervertebral medical device designed and/or manufactured by or for Integra.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.10. "Integra Customers" means End Users who purchase Treated Integra Products and Partially Treated Integra Products from Integra or its Affiliates and does include any Integra Affiliates. "Integra Products" means any implantable spinal surgery interbody and/or intervertebral medical device designed and/or manufactured by Integra subcontractors.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Integra Customers" is changed to "End Users" and modified to include Integra Affiliates, contradicting the original exclusion. This creates uncertainty regarding who qualifies as a customer for royalty calculations and exclusivity provisions. Additionally, the manufacturing of "Integra Products" is modified to indicate that it is done by Integra subcontractors rather than Integra itself, this generates questions about the warranty liabilities and product responsibilities. These changes directly conflict with sections dealing with Net Sales calculations and liability for product defects.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.17. "Net Sales" means the gross amount of all revenues invoiced and received by Integra and its Affiliates from Integra Customers from the Sale of Treated Integra Products and Partially Treated Integra Products, less the following deductions (to the extent otherwise then or previously included in the gross amounts invoiced and in respect of which no previous deduction was taken): (i) amounts taken or accrued for sales, distributor or other commissions allowed, discounts allowed dealers, trade and/or quantity and cash discounts; (ii) refunds, rebates, chargebacks, replacements or credits and allowances actually allowed or granted to purchasers on account of contractual obligations, rejections, returns, or billing errors and for uncollectible amounts (except to the extent later collected) on Sales; (iii) sales, use and/or other excise taxes, import and/or export duties paid, tariffs, and any other governmental tax or charge (except income taxes) imposed on or at the time of production, importation, use, or sale of the Treated Integra Product or Partially Treated Integra Product, including any value added taxes, and taxes on medical devices; (iv) shipping insurance costs and prepaid transportation and/or freight charges. Net Sales shall exclude any amounts Integra or its Affiliates receive for Treated Integra Product or Partially Treated Integra Product that are used for clinical trials required or reasonably deemed to be desirable for Regulatory Approval or additional product indications in any country.
perturbation.justification: The modification to the definition of "Integra Customers" to include "Integra Affiliates" directly contradicts section 1.17, which defines "Net Sales" as revenue received from "Integra Customers." The inclusion of Integra Affiliates in the definition of "Integra Customers" in the changed text means that sales to affiliates would now be included in Net Sales calculations, whereas previously, those affiliate sales may have been treated differently for royalty purposes or other financial calculations. Additionally, changing the manufacturing of "Integra Products" from Integra to Integra subcontractors creates uncertainty about liability for product defects. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because Section 1.17 depends on a clear understanding of who the "Integra Customers" are for the purposes of revenue calculations. This change creates uncertainty regarding the revenues coming from 'Integra Customers' that would be included or excluded. The explanation states this creates questions about warranty liabilities.

perturbation.original_text: 1.29. "Treated Integra Product" means an Integra Product that utilizes or embodies, in whole or part, the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology, excluding Partially Treated Integra Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.29. "Treated Integra Product" means an Integra Device that utilizes or embodies, in whole or part, the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology, excluding Partially Treated Integra Product.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Integra Product" to "Integra Device" in the definition of "Treated Integra Product" creates ambiguity. This contradicts the definition of "Integra Products" in Section 1.10, which defines it as "any implantable spinal surgery interbody and/or intervertebral medical device designed and/or manufactured by or for Integra." By introducing "Integra Device" without defining it, the contract becomes unclear whether "Integra Device" is synonymous with "Integra Product" or refers to something different. This creates uncertainty in various parts of the contract that use the term "Treated Integra Product", especially regarding royalty calculations and exclusivity obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2. Fees. (a) Treated Integra Products. Subject to Section 2.2(c), for so long as the Agreement has not been converted to a non-exclusive arrangement under the provisions of Section 3.2, Integra shall pay PcoMed a Fee of ***% of Net Sales of all Treated Integra Product Sold by Integra or its Affiliates. Subject to Section 2.2(c), for so long as Integra's Rights under the Agreement have been converted to a non-exclusive arrangement under the provisions of Section 3.2, Integra shall pay PcoMed a Fee of ***% of Net Sales of all Treated Integra Product Sold by Integra or its Affiliates. The Fee rate payable shall be determined based on whether this Agreement is exclusive or non-exclusive at the time of Integra's Sale of the Treated Integra Product, not at the time of PcoMed's production of the Treated Integra Product.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Integra Product" to "Integra Device" introduces an undefined term that conflicts with the existing definition of "Integra Products" in Section 1.10. This directly impacts Section 2.2(a), which relies on the defined term "Treated Integra Products" to calculate fees. Because the definition of 'Treated Integra Product' now uses a term that is ambigious (Integra Device), it creates uncertainty in determining which products are subject to the fee, creating a strong contradiction in the document based on 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 2.4. Minimum Payments. Integra shall use commercially reasonable efforts to Sell Treated Integra Products and Partially Treated Integra Products that generate payments to PcoMed of no less than the Minimum Payments applicable to each Minimum Payment Period. The Minimum Payment applicable to each Minimum Payment Period shall be due annually on or before 45 days after the last day of each Minimum Payment Period.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4. Minimum Payments. Integra shall use commercially reasonable efforts to Distribute Treated Integra Products and Partially Treated Integra Products that generate payments to PcoMed of no less than the Minimum Payments applicable to each Minimum Payment Period. The Minimum Payment applicable to each Minimum Payment Period shall be due annually on or before 90 days after the last day of each Minimum Payment Period.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Sell" to "Distribute" introduces a different concept and affects Integra's obligations. While 'Sell' implies a completed transaction for revenue, 'Distribute' could imply mere shipment to distributors, potentially lowering the actual revenue generated and affecting the fulfillment of the Minimum Payment clause. Furthermore, changing the payment term from '45 days' to '90 days' creates financial risk and questions about cashflow projections. This is directly contradicting the agreed-upon period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1. Payment Terms and Reports. Payments due under Section 2.2 shall be payable to PcoMed by Integra on a quarterly basis within 45 days following the end of each calendar quarter. Each such payment shall be accompanied by a statement setting forth in reasonable detail (i) the number and type of Treated Integra Product and Partially Treated Integra Product sold and the Net Sales applicable thereto, (ii) a breakdown of all the components of Net Sales for the determination of payments due under Sections 2.2 (the numbers may be and type of products may be stated in the aggregate and not by customer and are not required to be detailed by geographic area unless Fee rates are different in different geographic areas). Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product shall be considered as being sold for the purpose of the calculation of payments due under Sections 2.2 when the payments for the Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product are received by Integra or its Affiliates from a Third Party. All payments to be made under this Agreement shall be paid in United States dollars. Net Sales of Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product and fees in currencies other than United States dollars shall be first determined in the currency of the country in which they are earned and shall be converted (for the purpose of calculation only) in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles for financial reporting in the United States.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment terms from '45 days' to '90 days'. The contradicted text states 'Payments due under Section 2.2 shall be payable to PcoMed by Integra on a quarterly basis within 45 days following the end of each calendar quarter.' This creates a direct contradiction regarding the payment deadline, making the agreement inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. Furthermore, changing 'Sell' to 'Distribute' in Section 2.4 directly contradicts the statement in Section 6.1 which reads 'Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product shall be considered as being sold for the purpose of the calculation of payments due under Sections 2.2 when the payments for the Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product are received by Integra or its Affiliates from a Third Party.' The change in terminology directly contradicts how the calculation of payments is defined and when payments are considered sold.
